findings.evidence: The PDF features a structured header with left-aligned official names and a centered state seal. The HTML stacks these elements linearly and breaks the visual grouping.

findings.description: Fragmented reading order with misplaced and duplicated header/footer elements.
findings.evidence: The HTML injects the footer DEQ logo and address above the main document title, followed by an unformatted plain-text duplication of the top header text, completely disrupting the logical flow.

findings.description: Loss of bold formatting on list markers.
findings.evidence: The Roman numeral agenda items (I, II, III, etc.) are bolded in the PDF but render with regular font weight in the HTML.

findings.description: Footer content interrupts the logical flow between document sections.
findings.evidence: A right-aligned footer text block and the DEQ logo are injected inline directly between the end of the agenda ('XI. Adjourn...') and the start of the 'WebEx Meeting Information:' heading.
